title: Occupational Therapist - Adults (Full-time/Part-time/Locum)
description:
Responsibilities

  • Conduct assessments and provide individualised occupational therapy interventions for adult and elderly clients with physical, cognitive, or psychosocial challenges.
  • Develop personalised therapy goals and intervention plans tailored to clients’ functional needs, interests, and life contexts.
  • Deliver therapy through individual or group sessions in centre-based, residential, or community settings.
  • Provide caregiver training and advice to promote independence in daily activities.
  • Collaborate with multidisciplinary team members and, where applicable, participate in case discussions to support holistic care planning.


Requirements

  • Bachelor’s or Master’s Degree in Occupational Therapy.
  • Fully registered or eligible for registration with the Allied Health Professions Council (AHPC), Singapore.
  • Passion for working with adults and elderly clients in community, residential, and home-based settings.
  • Experience in rehabilitation, eldercare, or disability-related settings is advantageous.
  • Fresh graduates with a strong interest in adult rehabilitation are welcome to apply.
  • Strong interpersonal skills, team-oriented, with a proactive and positive work ethic.


Professional Development Opportunities

  • Opportunities for professional growth through supervision, mentorship, and exposure to diverse adult clinical populations.
  • Support from experienced therapists and allied health professionals within THKMC’s multidisciplinary teams.


Join Us

At THK Therapy Services, you will be part of a committed team empowering adults and seniors to live meaningful, independent lives. Join us in making a positive difference in the community.

postedCompany.description:
THYE HUA KWAN MORAL CHARITIES LIMITED


THKMC was incorporated on 13 October 2011 as a charity to provide multiple social and welfare services to the community at large, and achieved an Institute of Public Character (IPC) status on 18 November 2011. THKMC is the charitable arm of Thye Hua Kwan Moral Society, which lives by the mission – To Serve Mankind – to help anyone who needs help regardless of their race, religion, colour, language, creed and culture. THKMC today serves more than 70,000 beneficiaries through more than 70 programmes across five Services, which include Disability, Early Intervention for Children, Family, Seniors’ Community, and Therapy.
